expectations.text: problems.md explicitly names 'utils.py' as a dumping-ground / generic filename problem
expectations.evidence: Section 1 'Generic / meaningless module name (utils.py)' calls it a dumping-ground module name.

expectations.text: problems.md calls out the generic class name 'Manager' as a naming problem
expectations.evidence: Section 2 explicitly calls out 'do_stuff(thing) on Manager — maximally generic'.

expectations.text: problems.md identifies __getattr__ / dynamic dispatch as a problem for AI agents
expectations.evidence: Section 4 flags DynamicAPI.__getattr__ returning a no-op lambda and swallowing typos.

expectations.text: problems.md flags the use of eval() as an anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Section 4 explicitly flags eval(expr) in run_expression as arbitrary code execution.

expectations.text: problems.md identifies the deep inheritance chain
expectations.evidence: Section 5 describes the 5-level chain User -> Auditable -> Persisted -> Entity -> Base as a deep inheritance problem.

expectations.text: problems.md notes that public functions lack type annotations / return types
expectations.evidence: Section 3 'Missing type annotations on public API' enumerates every untyped function.

expectations.text: refactored.py's public functions have type annotations on parameters and return values
expectations.evidence: identity(value: T) -> T and add_integers(left: int, right: int) -> int both fully typed; OperationRegistry methods typed.

expectations.text: refactored.py does not use __getattr__ for runtime method dispatch
expectations.evidence: No __getattr__ implementation; explicit OperationRegistry replaces it.

expectations.text: refactored.py does not call eval() or exec() on user input
expectations.evidence: No eval or exec calls in refactored.py.

expectations.text: refactored.py replaces the generic 'Manager' class name with something domain-specific (or removes it)
expectations.evidence: Manager is removed; no generic equivalent remains.

expectations.text: refactored.py's inheritance chain is at most 2 levels deep (or uses composition)
expectations.evidence: User composes an AuditRecord instead of inheriting; depth 1 from object.

expectations.text: rationale.md grounds recommendations in specific AI-agent failure modes rather than generic clean-code arguments
expectations.evidence: Rationale mentions agent reasoning about call graphs, LLM tooling, agent working memory, and silent typos.

expectations.text: refactored.py declares __all__ to make the public surface explicit (v1.3 guidance — Python-specific)
expectations.evidence: refactored.py lines 27-34 declare __all__ with six exports.

expectations.text: refactored.py uses @dataclass(slots=True) or @dataclass(frozen=True) to prevent ad-hoc attribute fabrication (v1.3 guidance)
expectations.evidence: AuditRecord uses @dataclass(frozen=True); however User itself is plain @dataclass without slots/frozen.

expectations.text: rationale.md or problems.md explains why slots/__all__ matters for agents (hallucinated attributes, import of internal helpers) — the v1.3 framing
expectations.evidence: Rationale mentions __all__ pins the public surface but does not frame it in terms of preventing agents importing internal helpers or hallucinated attributes via slots.
